name: Pays de la Loire (France)

colors.symbolism: the azure of the historic arms of Anjou and Maine
the silver / ermine field referencing the historic Breton heritage of the western Pays de la Loire

description: A field divided per pale: a blue hoist charged with gold fleurs-de-lis (Anjou/Maine) and a white fly charged with the ermine of Brittany.
history: Pays de la Loire is a French administrative region created in 1955 from parts of historic Anjou, Maine, Touraine, and Brittany. France traditionally does not promote regional flags, and this composite armorial banner is used in a de-facto capacity rather than as an officially adopted regional flag.

features.symbolism: A field divided per pale: a blue (azure) hoist half charged with the gold fleurs-de-lis of the historic counties of Anjou and Maine, and a white (ermine) fly half referencing the Breton heritage of the western parts of the modern region.

features.role: fleurs-de-lis of Anjou / Maine on the hoist, ermine of Brittany on the fly
features.symbolism: The two halves of the banner combine the heraldry of the historic provinces gathered in the modern Pays de la Loire region: Anjou and Maine on the azure hoist, Brittany on the ermine fly (a reference to the Breton-speaking heritage of Loire-Atlantique and the Vendee).
